title: AI-Driven Multi-Document Correlation for Financial Compliance - Varsha Shah, Independent
summary: A framework combines graph-based entity correlation, adaptive probabilistic risk modeling, and cross-jurisdictional normalization to detect fraud patterns across multiple enterprise documents. Evaluation on three million anonymized records across four jurisdictions demonstrates improved fraud detection accuracy, reduced false positives, and lower manual audit effort.
writeup: The talk presents a framework for financial compliance that correlates entities across multiple enterprise documents to detect fraud, combining graph-based entity correlation, adaptive probabilistic risk modeling, and cross-jurisdictional normalization. It matters because compliance work spans documents and jurisdictions where signals are fragmented and manual review is costly. Evaluation covered three million anonymized records across four jurisdictions, with reported gains in fraud detection accuracy, fewer false positives, and reduced manual audit effort. Core concepts include knowledge graphs, risk assessment, and cross-document intelligence.
